raised_in: K-reader steps 1-2 (pre-drafting sample review; 0 LLM spent)
doc: FableExperimentWorkOrder s3 WP-KEYS K-reader + s1.6 determinism conventions
rule: Chunk sample: '40 from chunks_manifest.json, h32-seeded, stratified by source_type proportional to corpus mix'
description: Three under-determinations/defects found in the literal v1 sample (sha a0090b90..., pinned pre-drafting, never consumed): (1) UNIVERSE ambiguity - 'chunks_manifest.json' covers the full 14-ticker frozen restaurant run, but WP-FA pins WING/CBRL/EAT/PZZA as 'unused in Phase 1'; the literal draw spent 13/40 chunks on those tickers. (2) NO TICKER AXIS - source-type-only stratification left 5 of the 8 F-C roster tickers (CAKE, MCD, CMG, SBUX, TXRH) with ZERO chunks, though the EXP-2-adopted reader's production use is exactly the 8-roster WP-FC-RUN build + EXP-3 candidate generation. (3) h32 RANK DEGENERATION - the 31-poly rolling hash is adjacency-preserving on shared-prefix strings: filenames differing only in trailing digits get near-consecutive hash values, so top-k-by-h32 selects CONTIGUOUS filename blocks. Evidence: every ticker's picks in the v1 sample are consecutive chunk files (QSR 004-008, YUM 033-039, DRI 056-059, SHAK 020-022, WING 016-019 + 061-063, EAT 045-047, PZZA 030-032, BLMN 020-022); one YUM 10-K (0001041061-24-000011) supplies 5 of the 40 slots. Effective document diversity ~15 documents, far below 40 independent draws; same-document consecutive chunks also correlate gold causes across records.
why_two_ways: (A) literal: universe = whole manifest, h32 rank, source-type strata only - faithful to the written words, produces the degenerate sample above. (B) purpose-read: universe = the Phase-1-relevant tickers, decorrelated rank, coverage floors - matches WP-FA's Phase-1 pins and EXP-2's consumer (the 8-roster FC-RUN reader + hold-out candidate generation). Both are codable from the current text; the sample is not yet locked and no drafting has run, so this is the clean amendment point (K-pairs v1->v1.1 pre-first-use precedent, here even earlier: pre-lock).
resolution: Fable ruling 2026-07-10 - recipe v2 PINNED (instrument design; no bars changed; nothing consumed the v1 sample): (1) Universe = frozen chunks_manifest rows with ticker in {CAKE, CMG, DRI, MCD, QSR, SBUX, TXRH, YUM, BLMN, SHAK} (8-roster + the two hold-outs; hold-outs are legal here - K-reader is a READER key, no catalog involvement, and the adopted reader also generates EXP-3 candidates from hold-out companies). (2) Rank r(f) = sha256 hex of ('K-reader.v2:' + filename), ascending - a DECLARED OPERATIONALIZATION deviating from s1.6's h32 convention for SELECTION sampling only (h32 remains fine for presentation-order shuffles, where adjacency is harmless; it is unfit for top-k selection over shared-prefix ids - see evidence above). (3) Document-diversity cap: at most ONE chunk per primary document (primary document = source_id of the file's max-content event, same event that defines primary source_type; A-variants folded). (4) Ticker floors: each roster ticker >=2, each hold-out >=1 (18 floor slots). (5) Source-type targets: Hamilton apportionment of 40 over the NEW universe's per-file primary-type mix. (6) Deterministic two-phase selection: Phase A floors - tickers in fixed order CAKE, CMG, DRI, MCD, QSR, SBUX, TXRH, YUM (2 each), BLMN, SHAK (1 each); within a ticker walk files by ascending r, pick the first floor-count files whose primary document is unused. Phase B fill - walk ALL remaining universe files by ascending r; pick f iff its primary-type count is below the Hamilton target AND its primary document is unused; stop at 40; if short, a second walk ignores type targets (document cap holds) and any such overflow is RECORDED in the protocol. Output sorted by filename -> K-reader.v2.sample.json, sha pinned in protocol s4 and in the eventual lock. K-reader.v1.sample.json stays on disk FROZEN, marked superseded-pre-drafting by this exhibit.
drafting_route_rider: Fable ruling same date (v1.8 scope rule - each key's drafting route decided at drafting time): protocol s1.1's Sonnet-only draft is REPLACED by a DUAL-TIER UNION draft - claude-sonnet-5 AND claude-opus-4-8 each read all 40 chunks independently at effort=high (~80 calls, within the WP-KEYS cap); records unioned at adjudication (judged-same-cause dedup); Fable prunes/adds every record as already pinned. Rationale: EVERY EXP-2 arm tier (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) is under test as a reader, so no candidate tier is a neutral drafter; a single-tier draft over-represents the drafter's findable set in the recall gold (relative bias FOR the drafter arm) and hides its blind spots; a two-tier union halves single-model blindness, and the residual both-miss class is covered by the EXP-2 all-arms-miss ra_* channel plus the Fable add-pass. Haiku is not added as a drafter: its unique valid finds still earn credit through EXP-2's judged precision lane, which scores candidate validity independently of the gold list.
evidence_files: keys/K-reader/K-reader.v1.sample.json (sha a0090b90fff9e58ae097423525f8419054341663b56d7dd8c09dcd87f4e551d1 - the degenerate sample, frozen as evidence)
keys/K-reader/protocol.md s4 v1 table (consecutive-run clustering visible per ticker)
proposed_amendment: None to the work order (protocol-level pin per the O13/binding-(d) precedent). One convention note for future keys: s1.6 h32 shuffles are for ORDER; any top-k SELECTION over shared-prefix ids must use a diffusing hash (sha256) or an equivalent decorrelation.
